sections.instruction: Questions 1-5: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C. Questions 6-8: Complete the notes below using words from the options given below. Questions 9-10: Complete the sentences below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.

questions.content: What does Peter want to drink?
What caused Peter problems at the bank?
options: A The exchange rate was down

questions.content: Who did Peter talk to at the bank?
Henry gave Peter a map of
What do Peter and Sally decide to order?

Options: Art gallery; Cathedral; Castle; Gardens; Markets
Art gallery
Cathedral
Castle
Gardens
Markets

sections.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B or C.

questions.content: The Counselling Service may contact tutors if
options: A they are too slow in marking assignments
B they give students a lot of work
C they don’t inform students about their progress

questions.content: Stress may be caused by
International students may find stress difficult to handle because
options: A they lack support from family and friends
B they don’t have time to make new friends
C they find it difficult to socialise

questions.content: A personal crisis may be caused by
options: A studying for too long overseas
B business problems in the student’s own country

questions.content: Students may lose self-esteem if
options: A they have to change courses
B they don’t complete a course
C their family puts too much pressure on them

questions.content: Students should consult Glenda Roberts if
options: A their general health is poor
B their diet is too strict
C they can’t eat the local food

questions.content: Students in financial difficulties can receive
options: B a loan to pay their course fees
C a no-interest loan to cover study expenses

questions.content: Loans are also available to students who
options: C can’t cover their living expenses

questions.content: The number of students counselled by the service last year was
The speaker thinks the Counselling Service
options: A has been effective in spite of staff shortages
B in under used by students
C has suffered badly because of staff cuts

sections.instruction: Questions 21-24: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS OR A NUMBER for each answer. Questions 25-26: Choose TWO letters A-E. Questions 27-30: Complete the table below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE TWO WORDS OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questions.content: What TWO disadvantages of the questionnaire form of data collection do the students discuss?
options: A The data is sometimes invalid
B Too few people may respond
C It is less likely to reveal the unexpected
D It can only be used with literate populations
E There is a delay between the distribution and return of questionnaires

sections.instruction: Questions 31-32: Choose the correct letter A, B and C. Questions 33-38: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THREE TWO WORDS OR A NUMBER for each answer. Questions 39-40: Choose TWO letters A-F.

questions.content: Corporate crime is generally committed
Corporate crime does NOT include
options: A employees stealing from their company
C fraud resulting from company policy

questions.content: The oil tanker explosion was an example of a crime which
options: B was not a corporate crime
F made a large profit for the company
